title: leucochrysum graminifolium
intro: Leucochrysum graminifolium, also known as Stinking Guinea Flower, is a plant species native to southeastern Australia, known for its distinctive and pungent odor. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Leucochrysum graminifolium are said to include relief from arthritis, muscle and joint pain, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Leucochrysum graminifolium is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or cream for topical application, and can also be used as an essential oil in aromatherapy.
